raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing to update my existing complaint regarding XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) and American Express, concerning a {$46000.00} charge made in XX/XX/XXXX. 

Since my initial filing, I have discovered new evidence that demonstrates clear fraud and forgery committed by XXXX and a lack of proper investigation by American Express. 

XXXX. Forged Contract Pages XXXX submitted documents to American Express that contain forged initials and altered pages that neither I nor my wife signed. These pages were never part of the original contract presented to us. My authentic initials differ clearly in shape and placement from those XXXX sent to Amex. This appears to be a deliberate attempt to fabricate consent and obstruct cancellation. 

XXXX. Fabricated Email Correspondence XXXX  also provided American Express with email communications that were never received at my email address. Some of these emails even include a misspelled version of my address, which proves they were created after the fact. I verified with my email provider that these messages were never sent or received. 

XXXX. Amex Mishandling and Failure to Investigate Despite these red flags, American Express closed the dispute in favor of XXXX, citing services rendered even though : XXXX own communication admits I never used any of their benefits. 
I paid separately for my own travel and hotel accommodations. 
I contacted Amex within 6 days of the purchase to cancel, well within XXXX  own stated 7-day policy. 

This demonstrates a failure by Amex to conduct a reasonable investigation under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ).

4. Pattern of Fraud Public records and online complaints show XXXX XXXX XXXX and related entities have a pattern of deceptive sales practices, forgery, and refusal to honor cancellation requests. I also found that the company falsely claims decades of experience but was only incorporated in XX/XX/XXXX in the XXXX. 

5. Requested Action I respectfully request that the CFPB : 1. Reopen or escalate this case in light of new evidence.

2. Require American Express to re-examine the dispute based on forged and fabricated documents. 
3. Investigate XXXX XXXX XXXX for systematic fraud against U.S. consumers. 
4. Coordinate with the FTC or State Attorneys General regarding LLVCs deceptive practices.

I can provide the forged documents, email evidence, and certified mail records upon request. 
This situation has caused severe financial and emotional distress to me and my family, and I appreciate your urgent attention to these new developments.
